title: fix: wan falls back to DashScope on airforce 429 rate limits
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Wan more try
gist.blurb: When the primary provider for the Wan video model gets too busy, we now automatically switch to a backup. Your video generation keeps flowing.
gist.summary: Implemented automatic fallback to DashScope for the Wan video model when the Airforce provider returns 429 rate limit errors.
gist.impact: Video generation is significantly more reliable during periods of high traffic.
gist.visual_concept: The concept is 'rerouting traffic'. The Wan model hit a roadblock (429), so we are diverting the flow to a clear path. Visuals: A switch track, a detour sign, or a backup generator kicking in.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot flipping a large lever on a retro control panel to reroute a glowing data stream. A chunky robot nearby holds a film reel. Lime green (#ecf874) and soft peach gradient background. Pixel text: 'REROUTING'. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic, Stardew Valley vibes.
